hypothesis: If FREED++ is trained with a dual-objective reward that (i) maximizes pharmacophoric similarity to a reference set of 53 estrogen receptor antagonists/degraders and (ii) minimizes structural similarity to existing compounds, then the generated molecules will display high pharmacophoric similarity to the reference set and high novelty (low similarity to ChEMBL/ZINC/PubChem) while achieving docking scores and QED values comparable to or better than the baseline, because the reward directs generation toward ER-relevant pharmacophores while avoiding known scaffolds without sacrificing drug-likeness.
